name: Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route
name_original: Mirador de Salón y Cabo Vidio — recorrido circular
description: Hike the moderate 7.0-mile Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route for panoramic views of the Cantabrian Sea and dramatic coastal scenery in Asturias.
region_intro: Hiking around Cudillero provides access to diverse landscapes, characterized by rugged mountains, green valleys, and a dramatic, rocky coastline. The region features prominent coastal cliffs, such as those at Cabo Vidío, which offer panoramic views of the Cantabrian Sea. Lush river valleys, like those of the Esqueiro and Piñera, contribute to the area's verdant scenery. Best hiking trails around Cudillero The most popular hiking route is Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the dramatic coastline, offering expansive views of the Cantabrian Sea. Another top favourite among local hikers is Coastal route from Nuviana, an easy 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This trail provides scenic coastal views and is often completed in about 1 hour 26 minutes. Local hikers also love the Cudillero Viewpoints Route, a 2.0 miles (3.2 km) trail leading through the village's unique layout to various miradors, often completed in about 54 minutes. Hiking around Cudillero is defined by dramatic coastal cliffs, green river valleys, and traditional mountain pastures. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy village walks to moderate coastal paths. The routes in Cudillero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Cudillero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Cudillero?
A: Cudillero offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 70 trails recorded on komoot. These range from easy strolls through the village to more challenging coastal paths, ensuring options for all ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cudillero?
A: The Asturian coast, including Cudillero, is beautiful year-round. However, for the most pleasant hiking conditions, late spring (May-June) and early autumn (September-October) are ideal. During these months, the weather is typically mild, and the landscapes are vibrant, offering stunning views of the Cantabrian Sea and lush green valleys.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cudillero?
A: The hiking trails in Cudillero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic coastal cliffs, the charming village atmosphere, and the diverse landscapes that combine sea views with green river valleys and traditional mountain pastures.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Cudillero?
A: Yes, Cudillero has plenty of easy options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343876872/coastal-route-from-nuviana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal route from Nuviana&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.0-mile path offering scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1338837797/cudillero-viewpoints-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cudillero Viewpoints Route&lt;/a&gt; is another easy 2.0-mile trail that explores the village's unique layout and various miradors, perfect for a leisurely family walk.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Cudillero?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048074190/cudillero-palacio-de-selgas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cudillero, Palacio de Selgas — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which is an easy 6.3 km (3.9 miles) trail. The Cudillero Tunnel also features in an easy 2.0-mile circular route, offering panoramic views of the village and the Cantabrian Sea.

Q: Are there dog-friendly hiking options in Cudillero?
A: Many of Cudillero's outdoor trails are suitable for dogs, especially the coastal paths and less crowded routes. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in natural areas and near cliffs, and to be mindful of local regulations regarding pets on beaches or in specific protected zones.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in Cudillero?
A: Yes, the region around Cudillero offers natural attractions including waterfalls. You can explore the area to find features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/865581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nonaya Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a rewarding sight during or after periods of rain, adding to the lush scenery of the river valleys.

Q: What kind of scenic viewpoints can I expect on hikes in Cudillero?
A: Cudillero is famous for its breathtaking viewpoints. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1338837797/cudillero-viewpoints-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cudillero Viewpoints Route&lt;/a&gt; lead to miradors such as La Garita, El Picu, and La Atalaya, offering unparalleled photographic opportunities of the village, its harbor, and the sea. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1338811952/cabo-vidio-cliffs-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route&lt;/a&gt; also provides stunning panoramic views of the Cantabrian Sea from dramatic coastal cliffs.

Q: What unique natural features or landmarks can I see along Cudillero's trails?
A: Hiking around Cudillero allows you to discover diverse natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1338811952/cabo-vidio-cliffs-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4999971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidio Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and the Cueva de la Iglesiona, a cave sculpted by wave erosion. You can also visit beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/93692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa del Silencio&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3958024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Aguilar&lt;/a&gt;, and explore traditional mountain pastures known as 'brañas'.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Cudillero?
A: While many trails are moderate or easy, Cudillero does offer more challenging options, with 6 difficult routes available on komoot. These often involve greater elevation changes or longer distances, particularly along sections of the Camino del Norte or routes that venture into the rugged inland areas. Always check the route details for specific difficulty ratings.

Q: How can I access trailheads in Cudillero using public transport?
A: Cudillero is generally well-connected by regional bus services, which can provide access to the village itself and some nearby towns. From these points, many trailheads are accessible on foot. For specific routes, it's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes to plan your journey to the starting points of your chosen hikes.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Cudillero?
A: Parking is available in and around Cudillero village, particularly near the harbor and designated public parking areas. For trailheads outside the main village, smaller parking spots or roadside parking might be available. It's recommended to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot, and always park responsibly.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. Cudillero is rich in history and culture. A pleasant route leads to the mysterious Hermitage of Santa Ana de Montarés, just five kilometers from the village. Sections of the Camino de Santiago (Camino del Norte) also pass through the area, offering a historical pilgrimage experience. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selgas Palace and the Jesús Nazareno Church‑Pantheon&lt;/a&gt; are notable historical sites nearby.
tips: Beautiful viewpoint to contemplate the Cantabrian and appreciate the views. It has a famous bench where everyone loves to take a photo. In the early hours of the morning there are usually no people and it is amazing to sit there for a little while.
Although Peña Doria beach is an isolated beach, during low tide it connects with La Cueva beach. It should be noted that it is a beach with strong waves, and swimming can be dangerous.
Simple path, you can go down to the beach and enjoy the turquoise water.
Inaugurated in 1950, it is located in a walled area, with two independent buildings, on the edge of impressive cliffs almost 100 meters high.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route?
faqs.answer: The Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route is generally considered moderate. Many sections are easy and suitable for most skill levels, particularly circular routes around 10 kilometers with minimal elevation changes. However, some areas can be more challenging, especially descents to beaches or adventurous spots like La Iglesiona, which requires specialized gear and guides.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The nearby village of Oviñana is a convenient starting or ending point for hikes along the Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route. You can typically find parking in or near Oviñana, which also offers amenities like fresh seafood restaurants.

faqs.question: Is the Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route is generally dog-friendly. However, given the rugged coastal paths and sections with loose stone, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near cliff edges, and ensure they are comfortable with varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route is typically during spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) when the weather is milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, but it may be busier. Always check the weather forecast before heading out, as coastal conditions can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in this area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route. It is located within the Paisaje Protegido de la Costa Occidental, a protected landscape, but access for hiking is generally free and open to the public. Please respect local regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several spectacular points of interest. You'll encounter the famous Sablón Viewpoint, known for its 'bank at the end of the world' offering 180-degree views. Other highlights include Peña Doria Beach, dramatic Views of the cliffs at Cabo Vidio, and the prominent Vidio Lighthouse. You might also spot other picturesque beaches like Vallina and Cueva.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, featuring coastal paths along towering, rugged cliffs that plunge into the Cantabrian Sea. You'll find a mix of easily accessible sections, areas with loose stone, and descents to various beaches. Green meadows and charming rural houses also dot the landscape, particularly around Oviñana.

faqs.question: Are there any unique geological features or hidden gems to look out for?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The cliffs themselves are a geological marvel, revealing impressive strata of quartzite and slate. A notable hidden gem is 'La Iglesiona,' a large sea cave beneath the lighthouse, known for its cathedral-like vaulting. Accessing it is challenging, requiring ropes and guides, and is only safe at low tide, making it an adventurous highlight for some.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the nearby village of Oviñana is well-known for its fresh seafood and charming rural atmosphere. It's an excellent place to find cafes and restaurants to refuel before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail, or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The specific route described here is a circular route, meaning it's a loop trail. This allows you to experience varied scenery without retracing your steps. There are also options for shorter out-and-back sections if you prefer.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on the Cabo Vidio Cliffs Route?
faqs.answer: For this hike, pack sturdy hiking shoes suitable for varied terrain, including potentially loose stone. Bring layers of clothing as coastal weather can be unpredictable, rain gear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), plenty of water, and snacks. A camera is highly recommended for the stunning views, and binoculars can enhance wildlife spotting.

waypoints.name: Views of the cliffs at Cabo Vidio
